var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

args.code:
import json

# Get all businesses from MongoDB
business_list = var_functions.query_db:28
if isinstance(business_list, str):
    with open(business_list, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = business_list

# Get rating data from DuckDB
rating_list = var_functions.query_db:24
if isinstance(rating_list, str):
    with open(rating_list, 'r') as f:
        all_ratings = json.load(f)
else:
    all_ratings = rating_list
    
print('Stats:')
print(f'All businesses: {len(all_businesses)}')
print(f'All ratings: {len(all_ratings)}')

# Check the structure
if all_businesses:
    print(f'Business sample keys: {list(all_businesses[0].keys())}')
if all_ratings:
    print(f'Rating sample keys: {list(all_ratings[0].keys())}')

# Identify businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
for business in all_businesses:
    attrs = business.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_businesses.append(business)

print(f'Businesses accepting credit cards: {len(credit_card_businesses)}')

# Extract categories from descriptions
from collections import Counter

all_categories = []
for business in credit_card_businesses:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Categories are in the format: "...services in X, Y, and Z..." or "...including X, Y, and Z..."
    categories = []
    
    # Try different patterns
    if 'services in' in desc:
        parts = desc.split('services in')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            categories_part = parts[1]
            # Remove "including" if it's there
            if 'including' in categories_part:
                categories_part = categories_part.split('including')[1]
            # Clean up the end
            if '.' in categories_part:
                categories_part = categories_part.split('.')[0]
                
            # Split by commas and and
            for cat in categories_part.split(','):
                cat = cat.strip()
                if ' and ' in cat:
                    categories.extend([c.strip() for c in cat.split(' and ') if c.strip()])
                elif cat:
                    categories.append(cat)
                    
    elif 'including' in desc:
        parts = desc.split('including')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            categories_part = parts[1]
            if '.' in categories_part:
                categories_part = categories_part.split('.')[0]
                
            for cat in categories_part.split(','):
                cat = cat.strip()
                if ' and ' in cat:
                    categories.extend([c.strip() for c in cat.split(' and ') if c.strip()])
                elif cat:
                    categories.append(cat)
    
    all_categories.extend(categories)
    
# Clean up categories
all_categories = [cat for cat in all_categories if cat and cat != '']

# Count occurrences
category_counts = Counter(all_categories)

print(f'Categories found: {len(category_counts)}')
print(f'Top categories: {category_counts.most_common(10)}')

# Check which businesses have ratings
business_ids = [b['business_id'].replace('businessid_', '') for b in credit_card_businesses]
rating_ref_ids = [r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', '') for r in all_ratings]

print(f'Business IDs from Mongo: {business_ids}')
print(f'Have ratings for: {[id for id in business_ids if id in rating_ref_ids]}')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'total_credit_card_biz': len(credit_card_businesses), 'top_categories': dict(category_counts.most_common(10)), 'business_ids': business_ids}))

import json

# Get all businesses from MongoDB
business_list = var_functions.query_db:28
if isinstance(business_list, str):
    with open(business_list, 'r') as f:
        all_businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    all_businesses = business_list

# Get rating data from DuckDB
rating_list = var_functions.query_db:24
if isinstance(rating_list, str):
    with open(rating_list, 'r') as f:
        all_ratings = json.load(f)
else:
    all_ratings = rating_list
    
print('Stats:')
print(f'All businesses: {len(all_businesses)}')
print(f'All ratings: {len(all_ratings)}')

# Check the structure
if all_businesses:
    print(f'Business sample keys: {list(all_businesses[0].keys())}')
if all_ratings:
    print(f'Rating sample keys: {list(all_ratings[0].keys())}')

# Identify businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
for business in all_businesses:
    attrs = business.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_businesses.append(business)

print(f'Businesses accepting credit cards: {len(credit_card_businesses)}')

# Extract categories from descriptions
from collections import Counter

all_categories = []
for business in credit_card_businesses:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    if not desc:
        continue
    
    # Categories are in the format: "...services in X, Y, and Z..." or "...including X, Y, and Z..."
    categories = []
    
    # Try different patterns
    if 'services in' in desc:
        parts = desc.split('services in')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            categories_part = parts[1]
            # Remove "including" if it's there
            if 'including' in categories_part:
                categories_part = categories_part.split('including')[1]
            # Clean up the end
            if '.' in categories_part:
                categories_part = categories_part.split('.')[0]
                
            # Split by commas and and
            for cat in categories_part.split(','):
                cat = cat.strip()
                if ' and ' in cat:
                    categories.extend([c.strip() for c in cat.split(' and ') if c.strip()])
                elif cat:
                    categories.append(cat)
                    
    elif 'including' in desc:
        parts = desc.split('including')
        if len(parts) > 1:
            categories_part = parts[1]
            if '.' in categories_part:
                categories_part = categories_part.split('.')[0]
                
            for cat in categories_part.split(','):
                cat = cat.strip()
                if ' and ' in cat:
                    categories.extend([c.strip() for c in cat.split(' and ') if c.strip()])
                elif cat:
                    categories.append(cat)
    
    all_categories.extend(categories)
    
# Clean up categories
all_categories = [cat for cat in all_categories if cat and cat != '']

# Count occurrences
category_counts = Counter(all_categories)

print(f'Categories found: {len(category_counts)}')
print(f'Top categories: {category_counts.most_common(10)}')

# Check which businesses have ratings
business_ids = [b['business_id'].replace('businessid_', '') for b in credit_card_businesses]
rating_ref_ids = [r['business_ref'].replace('businessref_', '') for r in all_ratings]

print(f'Business IDs from Mongo: {business_ids}')
print(f'Have ratings for: {[id for id in business_ids if id in rating_ref_ids]}')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'total_credit_card_biz': len(credit_card_businesses), 'top_categories': dict(category_counts.most_common(10)), 'business_ids': business_ids}))

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:14.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
